page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-96 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 vCPUs, which map to 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running on an Intel Xeon processor at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 384 GB of dedicated RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio of 4.0 GB per core. Cache resources include 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and a 54 MB L3 cache. This server does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities and memory throughput. The instance achieves top 25% results in multi-core CPU stress tests, Geekbench multi-core benchmarks, and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top 25% for both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read and write operations. Cryptographic performance is particularly notable, with the Passmark encryption test ranking in the top 10%, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. Single-core performance remains in the average tier.

This instance is well-suited for highly parallelized workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, and database operations, where it demonstrates strong benchmark throughput. It is also capable of handling moderate machine learning tasks, showing strong prompt processing speeds for small language models, though text generation for large models falls into the lower performance tier due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. The resource density of this instance offers a balanced, cost-efficient profile for enterprise-grade general-purpose computing without the overhead of specialized hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-96 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 96 vCPUs and 384 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 48 physical cores, it provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but delivers strong multi-core performance, ranking in the top 25% for multi-threaded CPU tasks, memory bandwidth, and software compilation. Cryptographic execution is highly efficient, placing in the top 10% due to advanced instruction support like AES and SHA_NI. While single-core speeds and large-scale LLM text generation are average to poor, the server excels in highly parallelized environments. It is highly suited for enterprise web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded compilation workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-96 is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-96 is a general-purpose VM with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and strong multi-core and cryptographic performance.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose virtual machine delivering strong multi-threaded performance and balanced memory bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB RAM powered by a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon processor
Strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in the top 25%
Top-tier cryptographic execution utilizing hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI
Optimized for parallel workloads like web serving, databases, and software compilation
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
